axis: ELF re-bake — propagate ω-runner-2 main.hexa precedence swap into shipped Linux ELF + image

trigger.root_cause: rebuilt hexa-runner:23021b99 image (post ω-runner-2 perl removal commit) re-bundled CACHED COPY layers — those layers contained Linux CLI ELFs built BEFORE the main.hexa shasum→sha256sum precedence swap. Embedded shell strings in ELF kept calling bare `shasum`, which is not present in perl-less container.
trigger.noise_signature: sh: 1: shasum: not found (4× per hexa invocation, non-blocking)

sequence_problem_recap.step_1: this session #2b: rebuild Linux CLI ELFs from main.hexa-as-it-was-then (pre-runner-2)
sequence_problem_recap.step_2: this session ω-runner-2 commit 23021b99: edit main.hexa to use precedence pattern, edit Dockerfile to drop perl
sequence_problem_recap.step_3: this session image rebuild: buildx CACHED the old COPY layers → shipped stale ELFs in new image
sequence_problem_recap.fix: rebuild ELFs FROM the post-runner-2 main.hexa, then re-bake image

actions_taken: interp ELFs unchanged (hexa_full.hexa was not modified)
hexa tool/build_runner_image.hexa (cache invalidated by new ELF content hash)
docker rm -f hexa-exec; resolver auto-spawned new container on next call

image_inventory.size: 170 MB virtual / 37.2 MB compact (unchanged from runner-2 image — ELF byte size delta from precedence swap is sub-100 bytes)

verification.smoke_test: fn main() { println("runner-4 smoke") } via HEXA_RESOLVER_FORCE_DOWN=1 hexa
verification.verdict: CONFIRMED — noise eliminated, no functional regression

lessons.lesson_1: buildx COPY layers cache by content hash; if source artefacts (build/*.elf) didn't change between rebuilds, the layer hits cache even if other source files (Dockerfile, parent .hexa) changed. To force fresh layer when source changed: rebuild the artefact first.
lessons.lesson_2: perl-less container is now durable: main.hexa precedence pattern + image carries sha256sum → no fallback to absent shasum at runtime.
lessons.lesson_3: When making cross-cutting changes that involve compiled artefacts (interp/CLI ELF), the dependency chain is: source.hexa → ELF rebuild → image rebake → container restart. Skipping the middle step bakes stale artefacts into a 'new' image.

remaining_at_risk_sites.ω-runner-3_gate_hooks: 5 sites already migrated preventively in 4e711d78 hexa-lang commit; never fired on container hot path so no noise to remove there
remaining_at_risk_sites.ω-bridge-9_atlas_dsl_v2_regression: tool/atlas_dsl_v2_regression.sh:50 — same wrapper-default pattern as bridge-8, but fails loudly via -x guard so lower urgency
